title: 10 best herbal essential oils for inflamed mouth
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for inflamed mouth are concentrated plant extracts that have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, making them an effective natural remedy for soothing and calming inflamed mouths. These oils help reduce swelling, ease pain, and promote healing by penetrating the oral tissues and gums. Examples of herbal essential oils that help with inflamed mouth include peppermint, clove, and tea tree oil, which have been shown to alleviate symptoms such as bleeding gums, soreness, and bad breath. By using these oils, individuals can experience relief from discomfort and improve their overall oral health, leading to a significant improvement in their quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with inflamed mouth because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the affected area, peppermint oil's menthol compound helps reduce swelling and ease pain associated with inflamation. Additionally, peppermint's antibacterial and antifungal properties combat the underlying causes of inflammation, such as bacterial or fungal infections. By reducing redness, discomfort, and infection, peppermint essential oils provide relief from mouth sores, canker sores, and other oral irritations, promoting a healthy and comfortable mouth.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and antiviral properties: Peppermint oil's antibacterial and antiviral properties help to combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause inflammation in the mouth, promoting healing and reducing discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in peppermint oil reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums, mouths, or throats, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a faster recovery.

Anesthetic and analgesic properties: Peppermint oil's anesthetic and analgesic properties help to numb the affected area, reducing pain and discomfort, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing mouth sores and inflamed tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm inflamed mouth tissues.
Barks: Contain salicylic acid, similar to aspirin, which can help reduce pain and inflammation in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours or until crispy.
Blend dried peppermint leaves with carrier oil in a 1:4 ratio and let it steep at room temperature for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate essential oils from solids and discard solids.
Store the resulting peppermint essential oil in dark glass bottles and keep refrigerated for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with inflamed mouth because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated tissues. The oil's antimicrobial qualities also combat bacteria and viruses that can exacerbate inflammation. Additionally, sage's antioxidant compounds help to reduce oxidative stress, a common contributor to oral inflammation. When applied topically or inhaled, sage essential oils provide rapid relief from mouth sores, gum inflammation, and other oral health issues, promoting overall comfort and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Sage oil's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi that cause oral infections and inflammation, reducing the risk of complications like abscesses and periodontal disease.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in sage oil reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed mouth tissues, making it an effective natural treatment for conditions like gingivitis and periodontitis.
Antioxidant properties: Sage oil's antioxidant activity helps neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues, reducing the risk of chronic inflammation and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in soothing inflamed mouth tissues.
Barks: The bark of the sage plant contains a high concentration of salvene, a compound that helps reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves from a trusted source or purchase dried herbs in bulk for this recipe.
Combine one pound of dried salvia officinalis leaves with four ounces of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Place the glass jar in a sunny windowsill or under direct sunlight for eight to ten hours allowing the herbs to infuse into the carrier oil slowly.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids after four weeks of infusion time.
Transfer the infused oil to dark glass bottles and store in the refrigerator to preserve the herbal essence for up to six months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with inflamed mouth because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, have been shown to reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by inflamed gums, cheeks, and tongue. Additionally, lemon balm's soothing effects can help calm irritated mucous membranes and provide relief from burning sensations often associated with mouth sores or ulcers. By applying a few drops of lemon balm essential oil to the affected area or inhaling its vapors through steam inhalation, individuals can experience significant pain reduction and improved oral comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal lemon balm essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth, providing relief from inflammation and discomfort.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of lemon balm essential oil can help combat bacterial infections in the mouth, such as gingivitis and periodontitis, which can cause inflammation and gum disease.
Analgesic: Herbal lemon balm essential oil has analgesic properties that can help numb the pain and discomfort caused by an inflamed mouth, making it easier to eat and speak.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm inflammation in the mouth.
Leaves: Containing citral and geraniol, these flavonoids can provide antibacterial and anti-inflammatory benefits to reduce pain and discomfort caused by an inflamed mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers and dry them in a warm dark place for three days.
Combine one part dried melissa officinalis material with three parts carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Steam distill the mixture using a clevenger apparatus at a temperature range of 100-110°c for four hours.
Separate the essential oil from the carrier oil by cooling and filtering it through a coffee filter paper.
Store the melissa officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle with an tight-fitting lid and use within six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen essential oils helps with inflamed mouth because of its anti-inflammatory properties that directly target the affected area, reducing swelling and pain. The oil's main component, methyl salicylate, has natural analgesic and antiseptic properties that help to soothe gum irritation and inflammation, making it an effective natural remedy for mouth sores, ulcers, and inflamed gums. Additionally, wintergreen essential oils can also help to reduce redness and promote healthy gum tissue growth, leaving the mouth feeling refreshed and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Wintergreen essential oil contains methyl salicylate, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in an inflamed mouth by neutralizing free radicals and reducing swelling.

Pain Relief and Anesthetic Properties: The menthol content in wintergreen essential oil numbs the affected area, providing temporary relief from pain and discomfort associated with an inflamed mouth, making it a natural analgesic.

Antimicrobial Properties: Wintergreen essential oil has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial growth and prevent infections that can exacerbate an inflamed mouth, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of further complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wintergreen roots contain high amounts of methyl salicylate, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe inflamed mouth tissues.
Rhizomes: The rhizome is the underground stem of the wintergreen plant, rich in menthol-like compounds that can help numb pain and reduce swelling in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves in late summer or early fall when they are most potent.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation or dehydrator at low temperature for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried leaves using a small-scale apparatus and water at a ratio of 1:5 for 4 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities.
Store the obtained gaultheria procumbens essential oil in dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid and use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower essential oils helps with inflamed mouth because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains compounds like beta-amyrin acetate and ursolic acid, which have been shown to reduce swelling and soothe irritated tissues. When applied topically to the affected area, it can help to calm the discomfort and reduce inflammation caused by conditions such as gum disease, mouth sores, or ulcers. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties can help combat underlying infections and promote a healthy oral environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Echinacea contains flavonoids and polyphenols that have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, which could help alleviate mouth sores and gum inflammation.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Echinacea help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammatory conditions like inflamed mouth.
Immune system support: Echinacea is known for its immunomodulatory effects, meaning it can help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation by modulating the activity of immune cells.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are used to create essential oils that reduce inflammation and soothe gum pain.
Flowers: The delicate petals contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help combat microbial infections and alleviate mouth discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea angustifolia roots and clean them thoroughly under running water to remove dirt debris.
Dry the cleaned roots in a single layer on paper towels or clean cloth for three days allowing air circulation.
Crush the dried echinacea angustifolia roots into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 15 minutes until desired texture achieved.
Combine one part of crushed root powder with two parts carrier oil like coconut olive or jojoba in a glass jar sealed tightly storing it at room temperature for seven days.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container discarding solids and transfer to dark glass bottle sealing tightly storing it in refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with inflamed mouth because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and fight off bacterial infections that can cause swelling and pain in the mouth. Additionally, thyme essential oils have antimicrobial properties that help to soothe and calm irritated tissues, providing quick relief from discomfort and promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Thyme essential oil has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections that can cause inflammation in the mouth, such as gingivitis and periodontal disease.
Anti-inflammatory: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, including thymol and carvacrol, reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with inflamed gums and mouth sores.
Antioxidant: Thyme essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the oral cavity from oxidative stress, which can contribute to inflammation and damage caused by free radicals.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain a high concentration of thymol, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in soothing inflamed mouth tissues.
Seeds: Thyme seeds contain a unique combination of fatty acids and terpenes that have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the oral cavity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves from a well-ventilated area with minimal pesticide exposure weighing approximately 100g.
Dry the harvested thymus vulgaris leaves using shade drying method for 7-10 days or until moisture content reaches 5%.
Combine dried thymus vulgaris leaves with carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a ratio of 1:4 and stir well to mix.
Allow the mixture to steep for 14 days at room temperature and away from direct sunlight before straining and filtering the liquid.
Store the final herbal essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and keep it refrigerated at temperatures below 45°f.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with inflamed mouth because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The oil's flavonoids and saponins work together to combat inflammation, while also exhibiting antimicrobial properties that combat bacterial growth and promote a healthy oral environment. Additionally, the oil's soothing and calming effects can provide relief from pain and irritation, making it an effective natural remedy for mouth sores and gum inflammation.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Calendula has antibacterial properties that can help combat infections in the mouth, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory: The plant's flavonoids and terpenes have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with inflamed mouth tissues.
Wound-healing: Calendula has been traditionally used to accelerate wound healing, including mucous membrane injuries in the mouth, by promoting tissue regeneration and collagen synthesis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Pot marigold roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with mouth inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, providing relief from mouth inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated, collecting enough for approximately 500 grams of dried material.
Dry the harvested calendula flowers using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 24 hours or sun drying for several days, until they reach a moisture content of less than 10%.
Combine 500 grams of dried calendula flowers with 1 liter of high-proof vodka in a glass jar, allowing the mixture to steep for 14 days while shaking daily.
Filter the infused liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids and reserving the resulting liquid as the hydrosol.
Distill the calendula hydrosol using a steam distillation apparatus, collecting the essential oil and water fractions separately, with an estimated yield of 2-3% essential oil from the dried flowers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with inflamed mouth because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, lemongrass oil can reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation. Its antiseptic qualities also help combat the underlying infection, promoting a healthy oral environment. Additionally, lemongrass's calming properties soothe irritated tissues, providing fast relief from discomfort. This natural remedy offers a gentle yet effective solution for addressing inflamed mouth issues.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and Antiviral Properties: Lemongrass essential oil has been shown to exhibit antibacterial and antiviral properties, which can help combat the underlying infections that may be causing inflammation in the mouth, such as gingivitis or periodontal disease.

Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Lemongrass essential oil contains anti-inflammatory compounds like citral and geraniol, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums and mouth sores, promoting healing and comfort.

Antioxidant Activity: The antioxidant properties of lemongrass essential oil can help protect the oral mucosa from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to inflammation and tissue degradation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemongrass roots have been traditionally used to treat oral infections and inflammation due to their antibacterial properties, making them a good choice for creating essential oils for inflamed mouths.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of lemongrass contain bioactive compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with mouth inflammation, making them an effective component in essential oil blends.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves from a local nursery or garden, selecting 50g of healthy leaves for distillation.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm oven at 60°c for 12 hours, stirring occasionally, to remove excess moisture.
Load the dried leaves into a stainless steel distillation apparatus, adding 500ml of distilled water and covering with a lid.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 hours, collecting the steam-distilled essential oil in a glass bottle using a condenser tube.
Allow the collected essential oil to settle for 24 hours before transferring it to a dark glass bottle, filtering it through a coffee filter to remove any impurities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram essential oils helps with inflamed mouth because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. When used topically, it reduces swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums and mouth sores. Its antioxidant-rich compounds also combat the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause infections, promoting a healthy oral environment. Additionally, wild marjoram's soothing qualities calm irritated tissues, providing fast relief from discomfort and inflammation.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic: Wild marjoram essential oil has antiseptic properties, which help to reduce the growth of bacteria and prevent infections in the mouth, thereby alleviating inflamed gums and mouths.

Anti-inflammatory: The oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce swelling, pain, and redness associated with inflamed mouth and gums, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing.

Antioxidant: Wild marjoram essential oil has antioxidant properties, which help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oral inflammation, protecting the mouth's tissues and mucous membranes from damage and promoting overall health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective against oral infections and inflammation.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids, which have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and heal inflamed gums and mouth sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh origanum vulgare leaves and stems at dawn when the plant is most potent.
Rinse the harvested material gently with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Steam distill the herb mixture using a 10:1 ratio of water to herb for 30 minutes to extract essential oil.
Separate the resulting essential oil from water by decantation and filter through a coffee filter paper.
Bottle the final product in dark glass amber bottles, ensuring proper storage in a cool place away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with inflamed mouth because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. It contains compounds like camphor, bornyl acetate, and cineole that have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth. Additionally, rosemary oil has antimicrobial properties, making it effective against bacterial and fungal infections that may contribute to oral inflammation. Its topical application can provide quick relief from inflamed gums, mouth ulcers, and sore throats, promoting a healthy oral environment and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Rosemary essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed mouths by blocking the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like compounds that cause inflammation.

Antimicrobial: Rosemary essential oil has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections in the mouth that may be contributing to inflammation by inhibiting the growth of microorganisms and promoting a healthy oral environment.

Antioxidant: Rosemary essential oil is rich in antioxidants, which can help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and tissue damage in the mouth, leading to chronic inflammation and potentially even oral diseases.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections associated with mouth sores.
Leaves: Contain camphor, borneol, and bornyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties to soothe gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the early morning for optimal oil content and potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours to reduce moisture.
Steam distill the dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves with distilled water at a ratio of 1:4 for 2 hours to extract essential oils.
Collect and filter the extracted essential oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to 6 months.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat an inflamed mouth with herbal essential oils is by mixing a few drops of peppermint, tea tree, and eucalyptus oils with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Apply the mixture to a cotton swab and gently rub it on the affected area several times a day. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of these oils can help soothe and calm inflammation, reducing pain and discomfort.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to inflamed mouth that are treated with herbal essential oils are gum inflammation, bad breath, and oral thrush. Certain essential oils like tea tree oil, peppermint oil, and eucalyptus oil have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help soothe and heal these conditions. For instance, tea tree oil's antifungal properties can combat fungal infections like oral thrush, while peppermint oil's cooling effect can alleviate gum inflammation and discomfort.
